commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=cc68503e4337c4314311e595f3e94f52ee17a092)
commands.review_summary:
The PR preflights the QMD probe cwd, adds a typed unavailable reason for binary versus workspace failures, updates doctor and memory-core diagnostics/tests, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main passes the resolved agent workspace as cwd into the QMD availability probe and renders every unavailable probe result as binary guidance, and a direct Node spawn probe with a missing cwd returned ENOENT.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus either a compatibility repair or an explicit maintainer decision on the plugin SDK type contract.

target.title: fix(memory): report missing qmd workspace cwd
target.body:
## Summary
- fail the QMD availability probe early when the configured workspace cwd is missing or invalid
- surface a workspace-specific doctor warning instead of implying the qmd binary itself is missing
- add focused coverage for the low-level probe, doctor message path, and memory-core caller

Closes #63158.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: When the configured QMD workspace cwd was missing, the availability probe could surface misleading qmd-binary guidance instead of identifying the missing workspace path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS rebased on current `origin/main` at commit `cc68503e43`, using the repo runner against the actual memory-host SDK, doctor, and memory-core paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts` and `git diff --check`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied terminal output from the local checkout:

```text
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
unit-fast: 1 file passed, 7 tests passed
commands: 1 file passed, 42 tests passed
extension-memory: 1 file passed, 33 tests passed
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 15.07s

$ git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: Missing/invalid QMD workspace cwd is classified as a workspace-specific unavailable reason, doctor surfaces workspace-specific guidance, and the memory-core caller preserves the more accurate failure shape.
- What was not tested: No additional gaps in the targeted QMD workspace probe and doctor behavior path; full repository CI covers build and regression breadth.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the patch, the same failure class was mapped through generic QMD availability handling and could imply the qmd binary was missing rather than the workspace cwd.

## Testing
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts src/commands/doctor-memory-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts
- git diff --check
target.author: sercada

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
